It belongs to the genus Calamagrostis, which includes various grass species native to different parts of the world. The Poaceae family, to which Calamagrostis azorica belongs, comprises a large group of flowering plants commonly known as grasses. intro: Calamagrostis azorica, also known as Madeira fescue, is a species of grass native to the island of Madeira in the Atlantic Ocean. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and studies have shown that it possesses various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.
